rulings.Case Title: GEORGE BERNARD CLEMENTS, JR., AN INDIVIDUAL VS. MILOUS JAMES IVORY, AN INDIVIDUAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 17, 2024, line 1. PLAINTIFF GEORGE BERNARD CLEMENTS, JR.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden. Motion for Summary Judgment and each issue for summary adjudication is supported by 15 facts. The facts are not supported by admissible competent evidence, e.g. 5, 9. As to the cause of action for fraud, there are no allegations in the complaint and no evidence submitted to support the element of "reliance." As to the cause of action for slander of title, no evidence is presented that Grant Deed was recorded by the defendant, while damages, including punitive damages, are sought from the Defendant. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT H. WONG ET AL VS. THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 17, 2024, line 2. DEFENDANT ZBS LAW LLP'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant's Motion to Strike Amended Complaint and Objection to Declaration of Non-Monetary Status is DENIED. Moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that a motion to strike is a proper mechanism to address: (1) "inherently deficient" causes of action whereas Plaintiff "cannot establish the necessary elements" of the claims because "the first amended complaint is premised on Plaintiffs' unsupported and erroneous conclusion that the foreclosing beneficiary, which holds the original wet-ink promissory note, lacks standing to foreclose;" or (2) objections to the declaration of non-monetary status because the facts giving rise to Plaintiff's Complaint led the Plaintiff to allegedly erroneous conclusions. CC 2924l(c) requires Plaintiff to set forth facts, which Plaintiff did, however erroneous the moving defendant believes that Plaintiff's legal conclusions thereon may be. Moving party heavily relies on Kachlon v. Markowitz (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 316, a case decided at trial. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: WILMINGTON SAVINGS FUND SOCIETY FSB, VS. DAGOBERTO FLORES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff?S Request For Admissions From Defendants And For Monetary Sanctions For $4,020.00 Against Defendants, And Their Counsel
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 17, 2024, line 6. PLAINTIFF WILMINGTON SAVINGS FUND SOCIETY FSB'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Admissions From Defendants And For Monetary Sanctions For $4,020.00 Against Defendants, And Their Counsel. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Further Responses is DENIED. Responses provided by the defendant are proper and sufficient re: their obligation to respond to requests for admissions as phrased. (e.g. RFA 1). Additionally, for further details/facts regarding the denials Judicial Council created From Interrogatory 17. (e.g. RFA 34). All of defendants' responses have now been verified. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
